text.tspan: Bit-Growth Management: In hardware, is often just a right-shift (>> N) to make the error very small
control before adding it to a wide, high-precision accumulator register. is then used to truncate or
scale that wide accumulator down to the required bit-width for the output control signal.
Unit Conversion: Sometimes the accumulator holds pure "Fractional UI", but the hardware
Phase Interpolator requires a specific integer code (e.g., 0 to 63 for one UI). acts
as the hardware mapping gain (e.g., multiplying the fractional UI by 64)
